title: 베이비 시터

goal.ko: 누군가를 돕거나 시킬 때 사동 표현을 쓸 수 있어요!
goal.en: I can use causative expressions to help or direct someone!

key_points: -도록 하다 — make sure to / instruct to

annotation.note:
끼다 = to get stuck / to be wedged in (happens to the subject)
머리가 끼었어 = My head got stuck
↔ 끼우다 = to insert / thread through (you do it to something)

입다(wear) → 입히다(dress someone) → 입혀 줄게
사동: 내가 너한테 '입히다' = I put clothes on you

script.speaker: lia
script.text: 자 이렇게, 셔츠 단추를 풀고 입어야지?
script.translation: Here, like this — you have to undo the shirt buttons first, right?

script.speaker: lia
script.translation: Should I put your pants on too?

script.speaker: aera
script.translation: No, I'll do it by myself.

script.speaker: lia
script.translation: Okay. I'll let you put them on by yourself.

annotation.note:
입다 + -게 하다 → 입게 해 줄게
혼자 입게 해 줄게 = I'll let you put it on by yourself
-게 하다 = let/make someone do something

script.speaker: aera
script.translation: Like this, put the pants on the floor.

annotation.note:
이러케 = 이렇게 (아라의 발음)
어린 아이들이 'ㅎ'을 'ㅋ'처럼 발음하기도 해요.

끼우다 = to insert / thread through (사동)
다리를 끼우다 = to put legs through (pants)
↔ 끼다 = to get stuck (자동, line 3)

아이 = 감탄사 (Oh my / My goodness)
아이 ≠ 아이(child). 어른이 기특함을 표현할 때 써요.

script.speaker: lia
script.text: 피나랑 로지를 잘 재우고 나오도록 해!
script.translation: Make sure you put Pina and Rozi to sleep properly before coming out!

annotation.note:
자다(sleep) → 재우다(put to sleep) — -우 사동
나오도록 해 = make sure to come out (instruction)
-도록 하다 = make sure to / see to it that

script.speaker: aera
script.translation: Rozi says she wants to play together.

annotation.note:
-대 = (someone) says that ~ (전달)
놀고 싶대 = (she) says she wants to play
= 놀고 싶다고 해 (full form)

script.speaker: lia
script.text: 그럼 로지는 데리고 나와서 맘마 먹여.
script.translation: Then bring Rozi out and feed her.

annotation.note:
먹다(eat) → 먹이다(feed someone) → 먹여
맘마 먹여 = feed (her) — 맘마 is baby-talk for food/meal

vocab.section: 🔄 사동 동사 쌍 Causative Verb Pairs

items.note: -히 사동. 입혀 줄게 = I'll put it on for you

items.english: to get stuck / to insert · thread through
items.note: 끼다 = to get wedged in (자동) · 끼우다 = to thread through (사동)

items.english: to sleep / to put to sleep
items.note: -우 사동. 재우다 = to put someone to sleep

items.english: to come out / to go in
to take care of, to babysit

grammar.title: 사동 접미사 — -이/히/리/기/우/구/추
grammar.explanation_en: Korean has 7 causative suffixes. The suffix you use depends on the verb — there's no strict rule, so learn them as vocabulary pairs. The causative form means "to make/let/help someone do something."

scene_example.english: I'll dress you (put it on for you).

rule_boxes.content:
먹다 → 먹이다 (to feed)
보다 → 보이다* (보이다 = 피동도 됨)
높다 → 높이다 (to raise)
속다 → 속이다 (to deceive)
rule_boxes.label: -이 사동

rule_boxes.content:
입다 → 입히다 (to dress someone)
읽다 → 읽히다 (to make read)
앉다 → 앉히다 (to seat someone)
눕다 → 눕히다 (to lay someone down)
rule_boxes.label: -히 사동

rule_boxes.content:
알다 → 알리다 (to inform)
살다 → 살리다 (to save/let live)
울다 → 울리다 (to make cry)
rule_boxes.label: -리 사동

rule_boxes.content:
웃다 → 웃기다 (to make laugh)
벗다 → 벗기다 (to undress someone)
숨다 → 숨기다 (to hide something)
rule_boxes.label: -기 사동

rule_boxes.content:
자다 → 재우다 (to put to sleep)
깨다 → 깨우다 (to wake up)
끼다 → 끼우다 (to insert/thread through)
서다 → 세우다 (to make stand/stop)
rule_boxes.label: -우 사동

rule_boxes.content:
돋다 → 돋구다 (to heighten/enhance)
(매우 드문 형태 — rare)
rule_boxes.label: -구 사동

rule_boxes.content:
낮다 → 낮추다 (to lower)
늦다 → 늦추다 (to delay)
맞다 → 맞추다 (to match/fit)
rule_boxes.label: -추 사동

items.en: Mom puts the baby to sleep. (causative)

grammar.title: -게 하다
grammar.explanation_en: -게 하다 is a longer causative form that attaches to any verb stem. It means 'to let someone do' or 'to make someone do' something, depending on context.

scene_example.english: I'll let you put it on by yourself.

rule_boxes.content:
혼자 하게 해 줄게. → I'll let you do it yourself.
쉬게 해 줘요. → Please let me rest.
먼저 가게 해 줘. → Let me go first.
rule_boxes.label: let someone do — 허용

rule_boxes.content:
공부하게 해요. → (They) make (us) study.
일찍 자게 해요. → (They) make (us) sleep early.
청소하게 했어요. → (I) made (them) clean.
rule_boxes.label: make someone do — 지시

rule_boxes.content:
짧은 단어 → 보통 접미 사동
예: 먹이다, 재우다, 입히다

모든 동사 → -게 하다 사용 가능
예: 공부하게 하다, 사게 하다, 읽게 하다
rule_boxes.label: 접미 사동 vs -게 하다 · 언제 쓸까?

examples.en: The teacher made (us) read the book.
Mom makes (me) go to bed early.
Please let me try it by myself.

grammar.title: -도록 하다
grammar.title_en: Make Sure to / See to It That — -도록 하다
grammar.explanation_en: -도록 하다 is stronger than -게 하다. It means 'see to it that' or 'make sure that' something happens — it expresses instruction or obligation.

scene_example.english: Make sure you put them to sleep before coming out!

rule_boxes.content:
제 시간에 오도록 해요. → Make sure to come on time.
숙제를 다 하도록 해. → See to it that you finish your homework.
조용히 하도록 해요. → Make sure to be quiet.
rule_boxes.label: instruction / obligation 지시·의무

rule_boxes.content:
-게 해 줄게 → softer, more willing
예: 혼자 하게 해 줄게 (I'll let you do it alone)

-도록 해 → stronger, more directive
예: 나오도록 해! (Make sure you come out!)
rule_boxes.label: -게 하다 vs -도록 하다 · 뉘앙스 차이

examples.en: Make sure to eat healthy food.
Please make sure to wake up early.
Make sure you get back safely.

pronunciation.title: 재워 → [재워]
pronunciation.rule: 자다 → 재우다 모음 교체 (ㅏ → ㅐ) — 불규칙처럼 보이지만 사동 형성 규칙

banmal_jondaemal.intro_ko: 사동 표현은 반말/존댓말에 따라 어떻게 달라질까요?
banmal_jondaemal.intro_en: How do causative expressions change between casual and polite speech?

rows.meaning: I'll let you / them do it alone.
Make sure to put (them) to sleep before coming out.

banmal_jondaemal.tip: 아이를 도울 때 써요 → 어른한테는 '드리다'로 높임 표현.
banmal_jondaemal.tip_en: When helping a child, use -줘/줄게. When the recipient is an adult (elder/teacher), use -드리다 to show respect.

adult_dialogue.title: 💼 어른들의 대화 · Adult Conversation
adult_dialogue.context_en: A parent talking to a babysitter / daycare teacher

lines.speaker: 엄마
lines.text: 저 없는 동안 아이 식사 좀 먹여 주세요.
lines.translation: Please feed the child while I'm away.

lines.speaker: 선생님
lines.text: 네, 걱정 마세요. 잘 먹이고 낮잠도 재울게요.
lines.translation: Don't worry. I'll feed him well and put him down for a nap too.

lines.speaker: 엄마
lines.text: 옷도 혼자 잘 못 입으니까 입혀 주셔야 해요.
lines.translation: He can't put his clothes on alone, so please dress him.

lines.speaker: 선생님
lines.text: 알겠습니다. 혼자 입게 해 보고 안 되면 도와 드릴게요.
lines.translation: Understood. I'll let him try by himself first, and help if he can't manage.

messages.text: 엄마, 아라가 셔츠 못 입어서 제가 입혀 줬어요.
인형들도 잘 재워 줘. 우리 리나가 진짜 베이비시터네! 😄

real_life.title: 📖 베이비시터 일기
real_life.passage:
오늘 엄마가 외출했어요. 나는 아라의 베이비시터가 됐어요.
아라가 셔츠를 혼자 못 입어서 내가 입혀 줬어요.
바지는 혼자 입게 했어요. 아라가 잘 했어요!
인형 피나랑 로지도 재워 줬어요.
나는 오늘 최고의 베이비시터예요.
real_life.passage_en:
Today Mom went out. I became Ara's babysitter.
Aera couldn't put on her shirt by herself, so I dressed her.
I let her put on her pants herself. Ara did great!
I also put the dolls Pina and Rozi to sleep.
I am the best babysitter today.

questions.question: 누가 바지를 입었어요?
questions.exp: '바지는 혼자 입게 했어요' → 리나가 입게 해 줬고, 아라가 혼자 입었어요.

questions.question: 리나가 인형들을 어떻게 해 줬어요?
questions.question_en: What did Lina do for the dolls?
questions.exp: '인형 피나랑 로지도 재워 줬어요' — 재우다 = to put to sleep.

self_check: 입히다, 먹이다, 재우다가 무슨 뜻인지 알아요.
'-게 하다'를 사용해서 문장을 만들 수 있어요.
이 에피소드의 대화를 보고 누가 무엇을 했는지 설명할 수 있어요.

quiz.question: 리나가 뭘 해 줄 거예요?
quiz.question_en: What is Lina going to do?
quiz.exp: 입혀 줄게 → 입히다 = to dress someone (-히 사동)

quiz.question: '자다'의 사동형은 뭐예요?
quiz.question_en: What is the causative form of 자다 (to sleep)?
quiz.exp: 자다 → 재우다 (-우 사동). 아기를 재우다 = to put the baby to sleep.

quiz.question: 리나가 아라한테 어떻게 해 줄 거예요?
quiz.question_en: What will Lina let Ara do?
options: 혼자 옷을 입게 해 줄 거예요.
quiz.exp: 혼자 입게 해 줄게 = I'll let you put it on by yourself. (-게 하다 = let someone do)

quiz.audio_text: 피나랑 로지를 잘 재우고 나오도록 해!
quiz.question: 리나가 아라한테 뭘 시키고 있어요?
quiz.question_en: What is Lina instructing Ara to do?
quiz.exp: '재우고 나오도록 해' = Put them to sleep and come out. -도록 하다 is a directive instruction.

quiz.question: '머리가 끼었어'에서 '끼다'는 무슨 뜻이에요?
quiz.question_en: What does 끼다 mean in '머리가 끼었어'?
quiz.exp: 끼다 = to get stuck (자동사). 끼우다 = to insert/thread through (사동사). 머리가 끼었어 = My head got stuck.

usage.heading: 🔄 사동 동사 완전 정리 Full Causative Table
usage.content: 능동(active) 동사와 사동(causative) 동사를 함께 외워 두세요!

examples.en: sleep → put to sleep (-우)
wear → dress someone (-히)
eat → feed someone (-이)
get stuck → insert/thread through (-우)
know → inform/notify (-리)
laugh → make laugh (-기)
be low → lower something (-추)
stand → make stand / stop (a car) (-우)
sit → seat someone (-히)

usage.heading: 🔀 -게 하다 vs 접미 사동 · 어떻게 다를까?
usage.content: 접미 사동은 짧고 자연스럽고, -게 하다는 모든 동사에 쓸 수 있어요.

examples.en: I put the baby to sleep. (suffix causative — natural)
I make the baby sleep. (also correct, slightly longer)
I make (them) study. (no suffix causative for 공부하다 → use -게 하다)
I'll let you put it on yourself. (-게 하다 = letting/allowing)

usage.content_ko:
같은 접미사라도 사동(causative)인지 피동(passive)인지 문맥으로 구분해요.
입히다 → 사동: 언니가 아라를 입혀요. (I dress Ara)
잡히다 → 피동: 말이 잡혔어요. (The piece was captured)
먹이다 → 사동: 아기를 먹여요. (I feed the baby)
먹히다 → 피동: 음식이 먹혔어요. (The food was eaten)
usage.content_en: Use the same suffix pairs, but context tells you: if the subject causes someone else to act → causative; if the subject receives the action → passive.

usage.heading: 🧸 아이를 돌볼 때 자주 쓰는 표현 (대본 외)
usage.content: A few more phrases that come up naturally while looking after a child, beyond the causative verbs.

examples.en: Stay still / behave.
It's nap time.
If you're good, I'll give you candy.
Why are you crying? What's upsetting you?
